introSection: Montu is a steel inverted roller coaster located at Busch Gardens Tampa in Tampa, Florida, USA. Built by Bolliger & Mabillard, it opened in May 1996 and was the park's second roller coaster built by the company after the success of Kumba in 1993. The ride opened as the world's tallest, fastest, and longest inverted roller coaster until 1997, when Alpengeist opened at Busch Gardens Williamsburg.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Montu was announced in August 1995. The ride officially opened on May 16, 1996, as part of a new Egypt area. It was the first inverted coaster with seven inversions, as well as the first coaster to feature an immelmann loop. It originally contained an alligator enclosed under the ride's first turn, with guests feet dangling above the alligators in the enclosure. This enclosure would be removed in 2000 after guests kept dropping items into the enclosure.

Montu was repainted in 2022 by Baynum Painting.

sections.title: Ride experience
sections.content: The ride begins with the floor retracting which leads to the front gate opening. The train leaves the station with a small dip and right turnaround out of the station tracking towards the tall chain lift hill. Once at the top, riders twist down to the left and into a  vertical loop, reaching speeds of up to. Following the vertical loop, the track passes through a tunnel and zooms into a immelmann loop, providing a foot chopper element with the pylon. After completing the immelmann, the train goes through a zero-g roll, where riders experience a feeling of weightlessness. Following the zero-g roll, the track hits a batwing, entering a trench at the valley between the two inversions. Leaving the batwing, the track rises up into the block brake. Following the second brake run, the track makes a dive to the right into a trench where it hits a tall vertical loop. The track then exits the trench, making a three-quarter clockwise turn. After passing under the zero-g roll, the track makes a left-hand turn over the first vertical loop's exit and dives into another trench to hit a corkscrew, before making another right turn onto the final brake run.

sections.title: Color scheme
sections.content: Montu has a navy blue track and yellow supports. Prior to its repaint in 2022, the supports were tan.

sections.title: Etymology
sections.content: Montu was the god of war in Egyptian mythology.

fullText:
Montu is a steel inverted roller coaster located at Busch Gardens Tampa in Tampa, Florida, USA. Built by Bolliger & Mabillard, it opened in May 1996 and was the park's second roller coaster built by the company after the success of Kumba in 1993. The ride opened as the world's tallest, fastest, and longest inverted roller coaster until 1997, when Alpengeist opened at Busch Gardens Williamsburg.

Montu was announced in August 1995. The ride officially opened on May 16, 1996, as part of a new Egypt area. It was the first inverted coaster with seven inversions, as well as the first coaster to feature an immelmann loop. It originally contained an alligator enclosed under the ride's first turn, with guests feet dangling above the alligators in the enclosure. This enclosure would be removed in 2000 after guests kept dropping items into the enclosure.

Montu was repainted in 2022 by Baynum Painting.



The ride begins with the floor retracting which leads to the front gate opening. The train leaves the station with a small dip and right turnaround out of the station tracking towards the tall chain lift hill. Once at the top, riders twist down to the left and into a  vertical loop, reaching speeds of up to. Following the vertical loop, the track passes through a tunnel and zooms into a immelmann loop, providing a foot chopper element with the pylon. After completing the immelmann, the train goes through a zero-g roll, where riders experience a feeling of weightlessness. Following the zero-g roll, the track hits a batwing, entering a trench at the valley between the two inversions. Leaving the batwing, the track rises up into the block brake. Following the second brake run, the track makes a dive to the right into a trench where it hits a tall vertical loop. The track then exits the trench, making a three-quarter clockwise turn. After passing under the zero-g roll, the track makes a left-hand turn over the first vertical loop's exit and dives into another trench to hit a corkscrew, before making another right turn onto the final brake run.

Montu has a navy blue track and yellow supports. Prior to its repaint in 2022, the supports were tan.



Montu was the god of war in Egyptian mythology.
